text.title: Banking Core — Data Residency Rules Procedure
text.summary: 6-step procedure to apply banking-core-data-residency-rules. Each step names its input, action, and output. Steps may not be reordered without explicit deviation note.

step.name: Inventory data classes
step.description: Input: system inventory. Action: Tag each class with PII / non-PII + jurisdiction-of-origin.. Output: Data map..

step.name: Inventory processors
step.description: Input: vendor list. Action: Tag each processor with its processing region + sub-processors.. Output: Processor map..

step.name: Map transfers
step.description: Input: Data map + processor map. Action: Enumerate every cross-border transfer.. Output: Transfer list..
step.note: Step 3. Required input: Data map + processor map.

step.name: Legal-basis check
step.description: Input: Transfer list. Action: Attach legal basis + TIA where required.. Output: Lawful transfer set..

step.name: Encode in IaC + alerts
step.description: Input: Lawful transfer set. Action: Block non-listed regions in IaC; alarm on unexpected egress.. Output: Enforced residency envelope..
step.note: Step 5. Required input: Lawful transfer set.

step.name: Annual review
step.description: Input: All artefacts. Action: Re-validate against current regulator guidance.. Output: Refreshed spec..
